# Echo

> Delivering CVE-free container base images that wow your scanners.

- [Container Security & Compliance Blog | echo](https://www.echo.ai/blog): Explore the latest trends and insights in container security and FedRAMP with echo's expert blog updates and resources.
- [Get Your Quote | echo](https://www.echo.ai/pricing): Instead of pricing per image, we use a value-based approach that gives you full access to our entire portfolio of secure-by-design container images.
- [Enterprise-grade Container Images | echo](https://www.echo.ai/product): Echo delivers secure, CVE-free container base images that auto-remediate vulnerabilities and streamline DevSecOps workflows.
- [About Us | echo](https://www.echo.ai/about): Echo is on a mission to eliminate container vulnerabilities at the source with secure-by-design, CVE-free base images.
- [Get a Demo | echo](https://www.echo.ai/get-a-demo): See how Echo's CVE-free base images can secure your containers, cut MTTR, and simplify vulnerability remediation.
- [Image Pulling - Eliminate your CVEs](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling): Deciding which container image or variant to pull? Browse through this guide to find out what images to use, when, how, and why.
- [Build With CVE-free Images | echo](https://www.echo.ai): Build with vulnerability-free base images that won’t break your app. Automatically patched, hardened, and FIPS-ed for you.

Containers, operating systems, and third-party dependencies are all swept through automated scanners designed to surface CVEs that could be exploited.

- [What Is Argo CLI Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/argo-cli): The argo-cli image provides a CLI to manage Argo Workflows in Kubernetes, submit, monitor, and automate pipelines with ease.
- [What Is Argocd Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/argocd): The argocd image delivers GitOps for Kubernetes, sync clusters with Git, automate deployments, and manage apps at scale.
- [What Is Bitnami-Kubectl Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/bitnami-kubectl): Bitnami Kubectl image provides a secure, portable Kubernetes CLI for CI/CD, automation, and cluster management without host install.

- [What Is Busybox Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/busybox): Learn how to securely pull and run the BusyBox image, view usage instructions, available tags, and access the full SBOM.
- [What Is Cadvisor Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/cadvisor): Cadvisor image tracks container CPU, memory, and network metrics, offering lightweight monitoring for Kubernetes and Docker.
- [camunda-zeebe](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/camunda-zeebe): Runs a distributed workflow engine for microservice orchestration and durable job execution, typically deployed as part of a Camunda 8 stack. [Docker Hub+1](https://hub.docker.com/r/camunda/zeebe/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
- [What Is Ceph Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/ceph): Discover what the Ceph image is, how to use it in containerized storage setups, and explore its available variants.
- [What Is Configmap-Reload Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/configmap-reload): Configmap-reload image watches Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Secrets, triggering app reloads for dynamic, zero-downtime updates.
- [Crane](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/crane): Inspects, copies, and manipulates container images and registries without requiring a local Docker daemon.
- [What Is Curl Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/curl): Learn how to securely pull and use the curl image, view available tags, explore usage examples, and access the full SBOM.
- [What Is Datadog-Agent Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/datadog-agent): The datadog-agent image collects metrics, logs, and traces, powering observability, APM, and monitoring in containers.
- [What Is Dex Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/dex): Dex image provides OIDC identity service bridging apps and IDPs, enabling SSO and secure access in Kubernetes environments.
- [What Is Dotnet-Aspnet Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/dotnet-aspnet): Dotnet-aspnet image runs ASP.NET Core apps with optimized runtime, ideal for production APIs, web apps, and microservices.
- [What Is Dotnet-Runtime Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/dotnet-runtime): Dotnet-runtime image runs prebuilt .NET apps with a small, secure footprint, ideal for console apps and background services.
- [What Is Dotnet-Sdk Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/dotnet-sdk): Dotnet-sdk image includes compilers, build tools, and runtime, enabling containerized .NET app development, testing, and CI/CD.
- [What Is Eclipse-Temurin Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/eclipse-temurin): Eclipse Temurin image delivers TCK-tested OpenJDK builds with JDK/JRE options, trusted for enterprise Java apps and CI/CD use.

- [What Is External Dns Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/external-dns): Discover how to pull and use the External DNS image securely, with tag details, usage examples, and a complete SBOM.
- [What Is External-Secrets Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/external-secrets): External-secrets image syncs secrets from AWS, GCP, Vault, or Azure into Kubernetes, centralizing management and boosting security.
- [What Is Falcosidekick Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/falcosidekick): Learn what the Falcosidekick image is, how to use it, and the variants available for routing Falco runtime security alerts at scale.
- [What Is Fluent-Bit Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/fluent-bit): The fluent-bit image runs Fluent Bit for lightweight log collection, parsing, and forwarding in Kubernetes, Docker, and edge.
- [What Is fluentd Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/fluentd): An image that normalizes heterogeneous logs into structured events and routes them to multiple backends simultaneously
- [What Is Fluxcd-Flagger Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/fluxcd-flagger): Flagger image automates Kubernetes progressive delivery with canary, blue/green, and A/B testing, reducing risk and improving rollouts.
- [What Is Fluxcd-Helm-Controller Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/fluxcd-helm-controller): FluxCD helm-controller image manages Helm releases declaratively, automating installs, upgrades, and rollbacks in GitOps workflows.
- [What Is Fluxcd-Kustomize-Controller Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/fluxcd-kustomize-controller): FluxCD kustomize-controller image applies Kubernetes manifests with Kustomize, enabling GitOps delivery across environments.
- [What Is Fluxcd-Notification-Controller Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/fluxcd-notification-controller): FluxCD notification-controller image sends GitOps alerts to Slack, Teams, GitHub, and more, enabling event-driven automation.
- [What Is Fluxcd-Source-Controller Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/fluxcd-source-controller): FluxCD source-controller image manages Git, Helm, S3, and OCI sources, enabling GitOps workflows by syncing cluster state.
- [Ghidra](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/ghidra): A containerized software reverse engineering suite for headless analysis, automation, and collaborative project workflows.
- [What Is Gitlab-Runner Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/gitlab-runner): GitLab Runner image executes CI/CD jobs in Docker, Kubernetes, or VMs, enabling automated builds, tests, and deployments.
- [What Is Gradle Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/gradle): Gradle image bundles Gradle and JDK for CI/CD, enabling fast, reproducible builds, tests, and automation for JVM apps.
- [What Is Grafana Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/grafana): The grafana image runs Grafana in containers, visualize metrics, logs, and traces with dashboards, alerts, and integrations.
- [What Is Grafana-Tempo Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/grafana-tempo): Grafana Tempo image offers scalable, low-cost distributed tracing, storing data in object storage and integrating with Grafana.
- [What Is Haproxy Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/haproxy): HAProxy image delivers high-performance load balancing and reverse proxy, with SSL, health checks, and Kubernetes ingress use.
- [helm-operator](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/helm-operator): Runs a Kubernetes operator, which manages Helm releases declaratively using custom resources instead of imperative helm commands.
- [k3s](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/k3s): Provides a lightweight, fully compliant Kubernetes distribution optimized for edge, IoT, and resource-constrained environments.
- [What Is K8s-Sidecar Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/k8s-sidecar): K8s-sidecar image syncs ConfigMaps and Secrets to volumes, enabling dynamic app configs without restarts in Kubernetes.
- [What Is Kafka Image? How to Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/kafka): Learn how to securely pull and use Kafka container images, optimize performance, and ensure reproducible builds using trusted sources.
- [Karpenter](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/karpenter): Runs a Kubernetes autoscaling controller responsible for provisioning and deprovisioning nodes based on real-time workload demand.
- [What Is Keda Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/keda): The keda image runs KEDA to enable event-driven autoscaling in Kubernetes, scaling workloads on external metrics and events.
- [Keycloak](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/keycloak): Provides a full-featured identity and access management platform supporting SSO, OAuth2, OIDC, and SAML for securing applications and APIs.
- [What Is Keycloak-Fips Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/keycloak-fips): The keycloak-fips image delivers FIPS 140-2 compliant Keycloak for secure SSO, OAuth2, and IAM in regulated environments.
- [Kong](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/kong): Runs a cloud-native API gateway used to manage, secure, and route traffic to services using plugins and declarative configuration.
- [What Is Kube-State-Metrics Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/kube-state-metrics): The kube-state-metrics image exports Kubernetes object state to Prometheus metrics for monitoring clusters and workloads.
- [What Is Kyverno Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/kyverno): Kyverno image enforces Kubernetes policies with YAML, enabling validation, mutation, and image verification for security and compliance.
- [What Is Kyverno-Kyvernopre Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/kyverno-kyvernopre): Kyverno-Kyvernopre image handles policy pre-processing in Kubernetes, boosting Kyverno performance and scalability in large clusters.
- [What Is liquibase Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/liquibase): Provides a database schema change management tool designed to help teams safely deploy database changes across environments.
- [What Is Minio Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/minio): Discover what the MinIO image is, how to deploy it in containers, and the available variants for scalable object storage setups.
- [What Is MLflow Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/mlflow): Discover MLflow Image for managing ML lifecycle, track experiments, serve models, and streamline deployment.
- [What Is Nginx Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/nginx): Find secure pull instructions for the NGINX image, explore available tags, usage examples, and access the complete SBOM.
- [What Is Nginx-S3-Gateway Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/nginx-s3-gateway): Nginx-s3-gateway image bridges HTTP to S3 storage, enabling static sites, media backends, caching, and secure object access.
- [What Is Node Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/node): The node image runs Node.js in containers, build and deploy JavaScript apps, APIs, and microservices with npm or yarn.
- [What Is NVIDIA-Container-Toolkit Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/nvidia-container-toolkit): Enable GPU access in containers with the NVIDIA-container-toolkit image, run CUDA apps in Docker or Kubernetes with ease.
- [What Is Nvidia-K8s-Device-Plugin Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/nvidia-k8s-device-plugin): NVIDIA K8s device plugin image exposes GPU resources to Kubernetes, enabling ML, AI, and HPC workloads with GPU acceleration.
- [What Is OpenJDK Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/openjdk): The openjdk image runs and builds Java apps in containers, offering JRE, compiler, and libraries across multiple Java versions.
- [What Is openresty Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/openresty): Provides a high-performance web platform built on NGINX and LuaJIT, designed for dynamic content, routing, and API gateway workloads.
- [What Is Opentelemetry-Collector-Contrib Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/opentelemetry-collector-contrib): Learn how the opentelemetry-collector-contrib image extends observability pipelines with community receivers, processors, and exporters.
- [What Is Postgres Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/postgres): The postgres image runs PostgreSQL in containers, reliable, SQL-compliant database for web apps, analytics, and enterprise use.
- [Postgres-operator](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/postgres-operator): Manages the lifecycle of PostgreSQL clusters on Kubernetes through automation, high availability, backups, scaling, and day-2 operations.
- [What Is Prometheus-Adapter Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/prometheus-adapter): The prometheus-adapter image exposes Prometheus metrics to Kubernetes APIs, enabling autoscaling with custom and external metrics.
- [What Is Prometheus-Alertmanager Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/prometheus-alertmanager): The prometheus-alertmanager image manages and routes Prometheus alerts to email, Slack, PagerDuty, and other channels.
- [What Is Prometheus-Pushgateway Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/prometheus-pushgateway): Prometheus Pushgateway image lets short-lived jobs push metrics for scraping, enabling CI/CD and batch job observability.
- [What Is Promethues Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/promethues): The prometheus image collects and stores metrics with PromQL, powering monitoring and alerting in Kubernetes and cloud apps.
- [What Is Python Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/python): The python image runs Python apps in containers, supporting web, data, ML, and automation with pip and standard libraries.
- [What Is Rabbitmq Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/rabbitmq): The rabbitmq image runs RabbitMQ in containers, enable reliable queues, pub/sub messaging, and event-driven microservices.
- [What Is Redis Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/redis): The redis image runs Redis in containers, fast in-memory data store for caching, queues, sessions, and microservices.
- [Redis-operator](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/redis-operator): Automates the deployment, scaling, failover, backups, and configuration of Redis clusters inside Kubernetes.
- [What Is Rook Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/rook): Discover what the Rook image is, how to deploy it with containers, and explore its available variants for storage orchestration.
- [What Is Ruby Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/ruby): The ruby image runs Ruby apps in containers, supporting Rails, APIs, job processing, and automation with gems and bundler.
- [What Is Semgrep Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/semgrep): The semgrep image enables fast, CI-friendly static code analysis to catch bugs, security issues, and policy violations.
- [What Is sentry Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/sentry): An application monitoring and error tracking tool used to identify, triage, and resolve issues in real time across distributed sstems.
- [What Is Terraform Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/terraform): Learn how to pull and run the Terraform image securely, including usage instructions, tags, and SBOM for verification.
- [What Is Traefik Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/traefik): Discover what the Traefik image is, how to use it with your containers, and learn about its available variants and deployment options.
- [What Is Ubuntu Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/ubuntu): ubuntu image provides a lightweight, flexible base operating system used as a foundation for building and running containerized applications.
- [What Is Valkey Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/valkey): Valkey image is a Redis-compatible, open-source cache and data store, offering clustering, replication, and long-term availability.
- [What Is Vault Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/vault): Learn how to securely pull and run the Vault image, explore available tags, and access its complete SBOM for verification.
- [What Is Velero Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/velero): Velero image enables Kubernetes backup, restore, and migration with object storage, ensuring disaster recovery and portability.
- [What Is Wildfly Image? How To Use & Variants](https://www.echo.ai/image-pulling/wildfly): Discover what the WildFly image is, how to deploy it in containers, and explore its available variants for Java app hosting.
- [What Is Code Signing? Benefits & Use Cases](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/code-signing): Discover what code signing is, its key benefits, and practical use cases to ensure software authenticity and boost security.
- [Container Compliance](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/container-compliance): Container compliance refers to the practice of ensuring that container images, registries, and runtime environments adhere to predefined security, configuration, and governance requirements.
- [What is Container Hardening? Fuctions & Best Practices](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/container-hardening): Learn what container hardening is, core functions, and best practices to secure containerized applications in modern environments.
- [CVE Remediation](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/cve-remediation): CVE remediation is the process of eliminating exposure to known software vulnerabilities identified by Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs).
- [What Is the Cyber Resilience Act? Goals & Requirements](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/cyber-resilience-act): Discover what the Cyber Resilience Act is, its main goals, and key requirements for enhancing cybersecurity in digital products.
- [Distroless Containers](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/distroless-containers): Distroless containers are container images that contain only an application and its runtime dependencies, without including common operating system utilities such as package managers, shells, or debugging tools.
- [What Is a Docker Image? Anatomy & How it Works](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/docker-image): A Docker image is a portable, immutable software package that bundles everything needed to run a specific application or service.
- [Image Provenance](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/image-provenance): As containerized software moves rapidly through build pipelines, registries, and production environments, understanding where an image came from and how it was created has become a foundational security requirement.
- [What Is the Open Container Initiative? Specifications & Impotance](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/open-container-initiative-oci): Modern software runs on containers. They power microservices, CI/CD pipelines, Kubernetes clusters, cloud platforms, and edge environments.
- [What Is Software Composition Analysis? Best Practices & Importance](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/software-composition-analysis): Learn what software composition analysis is, why it’s important, and best practices for securing applications with open source components.
- [What is Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange (VEX)?](https://www.echo.ai/glossary/vulnerability-exploitability-exchange-vex): Discover how VEX adds context to vulnerability alerts, improves risk triage, and streamlines Cyber Resilience Act compliance.
